question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R0 (PE-A) adds an IS-IS HMAC-MD5 hello-password on its only data
interface (GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 → R2). R2 has no password configured.
R2 rejects R0's authenticated Hellos; R0 rejects R2's unauthenticated
Hellos. The Level-2 adjacency drops. PE-A is partitioned from the
SR-MPLS underlay — its prefix-SID 10 stops being advertised cross-core
and its loopback 192.0.2.10 disappears from R3/R4/R5 IGP tables.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.100 (R0, PE-A), an IS-IS HMAC-MD5 hello-password (secret123) was added to GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (R0's only core link, to R2) while R2 has none.
Because the two ends disagree on IS-IS Hello authentication, each rejects the other's Hellos as failing the authentication invariant, so the Level-2 adjacency cannot form on that link and R0 is partitioned from the SR-MPLS underlay - its prefix-SID and loopback 192.0.2.10 are no longer advertised into the other routers' IGP tables.
Fix: remove the hello-password from R0's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1.
expected_symptoms_summary:
%ROUTING-ISIS-5-AUTH_FAILURE_DROP reported by R0 (1 instance):
- R0: ISIS (LAB): Dropped P2P IIH from Gi0/0/0/1, R2 (0000.0000.0002), SNPA aac1.abc5.309a due to authentication TLV not found

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1
 hello-password hmac-md5 clear secret123

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1
 no hello-password

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:53:07.016 UTC: isis[1003]: %ROUTING-ISIS-5-AUTH_FAILURE_DROP : ISIS (LAB): Dropped P2P IIH from Gi0/0/0/1, R2 (0000.0000.0002), SNPA aac1.abc5.309a due to authentication TLV not found
